title: Irregular Verbs and Gender in Spanish
description: Learn about the verb 'ir', its conjugations, and how to determine gender for nouns ending in 'e'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: We said that the word for 'always' was siempre.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Now, let's work with the verb 'to go', which is ir. We have 'vamos' for 'we go' or 'let's go'. How would you say 'he goes' or 'she goes'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Va. Good. How would you make that 'you go' (informal)?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vas. Good. To ask 'where are you going?', you say 'a dónde vas?'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿A dónde vas? How would you say 'they go' or 'you (plural) go'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Van. Good. The 'I' form is 'voy', as in 'I'm going on a voyage'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Now, the 'to' form 'to go' is ir. It's irregular because it combines forms from two different verbs. How would you say 'I want to go'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Puedo ir. Good. 'I must go' is 'debo ir'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Debo ir. For 'I have to go', we use 'tengo que ir'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Tengo que ir. How would you say 'I have to go now'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Tonight' is 'esta noche'. How would you say 'I have to go tonight'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Tengo que ir esta noche. 'Noche' is feminine, as indicated by 'esta'. How would you say 'the night'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: La noche. Words ending in 'e' can be masculine or feminine. For example, 'carne' (meat) is feminine. How would you say 'the meat'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: La carne. How do you say 'I don't eat meat'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No como carne. The word for 'and' is 'y'. How would you say 'I don't eat meat and I don't drink alcohol'?

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: no como carne y no tomo alcohol

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No como carne y no tomo alcohol. Good.

outro.text: You've learned the irregular verb 'ir' and its conjugations. Practice using them in sentences to describe actions.
Remember, nouns ending in 'e' can be masculine or feminine. Use context to determine gender.
Try creating your own sentences using 'ir' and other verbs you've learned.
